body.title: Build ADC queue rack device — queue_next MCP tool, no claiming
body.result: QueueDevice BaseDevice subclass + MCP server + 21 tests; commit d2230e4d
body.description:
Build a queue rack device in UnseenUniversity that IS the work ticket queue. All consumers (CC, Igor, Librarian) call queue_next(worker) → ticket JSON or None via MCP. No claiming — queue_next is atomic: it reads the next pending ticket for the given worker and marks it in_progress in one DB operation. Backend: reads/writes cc_queue.py's Postgres table (public schema, queue table).

Device shape:
- devices/queue/ directory with __init__.py + device.py (BaseDevice subclass)
- MCP tools: queue_next(worker: str) → dict | None; queue_peek(worker: str) → dict | None (non-atomic, read-only); queue_show(ticket_id: str) → dict | None; queue_list(worker: str = None, status: str = 'pending') → list[dict]
- No queue_claim tool — raises LegacyDirectClaimError if somehow invoked
- Shim registration so the device appears in the MCP manifest

**Affected files:** /home/akien/dev/src/UnseenUniversity/devices/queue/__init__.py (new), devices/queue/device.py (new), devices/queue/tests/test_queue_device.py (new), unseen_university/shim.py (register new device)
**Design rules:** BaseDevice subclass (CLAUDE.md); no TheIgors imports; OOP-first; datacenter_logs/queue/ for device logs
**Scope boundary:** build the device + MCP tools + tests; NOT update /query-ticket skill (T-query-ticket-mcp-switch); NOT remove cc_queue.py claim (T-adc-queue-no-claiming); both gated on this ticket
**Test plan:** queue_next returns ticket dict when pending tickets exist for worker; queue_next returns None when queue empty or no tickets for worker; queue_next marks returned ticket in_progress atomically (second call returns None); queue_list returns full pending list; integration test with real Postgres cc_queue data
